desc: btn1 has two click handlers that synchronously set the same label, so the first handler's text is immediately overwritten and never meaningfully displayed.
btn2's nested-function handler overwrites the text set by its first click handler, so clicking it ends with the unrelated nested-function message.

The label is stored in mutable module-global state even though all callbacks already capture it, creating unnecessary shared state and retaining the widget beyond its owning window's intended lifetime.
anotherGlobal is defined but never used, leaving dead module-level state that obscures the module's actual behavior.
The btn2 callback uses two nested functions solely to perform one label update, adding needless indirection and making the signal behavior harder to follow.
